So the green broad EFC play. OK, so the five key points of why the play is so good is that it is a scalable offer. The sale is not as difficult to do, which will allow you to have, you don't need to have a unicorn salesperson to crush it. It'll, point two, is it'll help you focus on your churn, because the only thing that you'll have is to worry about how much money you make is your churn. There really isn't a bait in switching more, because you really are giving them the six-week on for free, which I think is great. You'll have a limitless front-end leads, because the offer is still the best offer on the market. And last piece is it actually helps people in a bigger way, because ultimately, like, we all know that six weeks is not going to save anyone's life. But if we can get them into a year with us, we can totally transform their life forever. So that's kind of some of the key points of why this is cool. So if you look at how this transforms the business, there's really only two front-end offers, the six-week free, in terms of what you're marketing, the six-week free, and HTTP. So how does it actually work, though? So you do everything identically as a six-week free, except after you finish the accountability in the sales pitch, you just tell people, so there's two ways you can get this for free. Now, there's a couple of different versions of it. Guys have done it different ways. Ultimately, like, any of them will work. You can just finish it and then say, would you like to know the other way you can get this for free? And people say yes that way, or you can introduce the fact that there's two ways either way will work. And so you can say the first way is you can do the program on its own. And people drive from all over just to do this. The program's $6.99, and if you lose $25.00 six weeks, you give them money back. Same exact clothes as we normally have. Or the other way you can do it is you simply become a VIP annual member with us and get the whole program at no cost, just as a gift to you, just for becoming a member. And it's just $49.00 a week. And then this is the last piece. Which would you prefer to do? And you say prefer because it's an assumed close. We prefer people who just pick what their preference is and they like crossing, but then you just get them started. An assumed close has either or options most times. And in this option, it gives us that ability to do it, which I think is really, really powerful. And so in my life, the only times I've ever seen people get above 80% close rate on cold traffic is when there's an assumed close that's being done. And that's what some of the best guys are doing right now. They're closing 90% plus on this offer by just assuming the close. The best closes in the world on cold traffic, when both people know there's a sale, is usually between 70% and 80%. Those are the best guys. If you go above 80%, 85%, usually it's because it's a different type of sale, meaning the premise of the sale or the offer is different, which is what this does to the offer. The Green Bride makes a six-week challenge more appetizing because now we're saying we're giving you $700 to $800 for the value as a gift, which also switches the relationship to you just being a total giver, which I love, from a business standpoint. And you're just getting the EFT. And yes, it's assumed as an annual membership. And yeah, I mean, this is me just kind of explaining this in a little bit more. It's just an easier sale. And I think it's a superior alternative. And this is all I would be doing as long as I didn't need the cash. So Joe is the one who did it. And when I was explaining this, one of the other pieces that he adds on is that, and if you do the membership option that we do to keep you kind of invested, is that as you do the challenge, what you'll do is you earn which is for every pound you lose, we'll actually give you $1 off your membership for life. So if you lose 20 pounds in your first six weeks, then it means that you will be able to stay at, it's going to be $20 off. It's $180 a month instead of $200 a month. You lose 40 pounds and it's $160. So it's still motivation for them to do well in the program. And earn as you burn is the phrase that he uses. If you have a very, very hard sale for whatever reason, you can tell them that you'll give them a 30 day, money back guarantee that they can get out if they don't like it. That is an absolute last case scenario. I would not bring that up unless it was absolutely necessary. But primarily, you're saying you go through the entire challenge pitch, you say, awesome, way the challenge works is you can do the challenge at $6.99, get the goal, you get it back as credit towards staying. So we actually do the whole six weeks for free and then you can use that credit towards whatever program you want, which is really cool. Or do you want to know the other way you can get it for free? Sure. Say like, if you just become a VIP annual member with us, we'll just give you the program upfront for free, no cost. And then what you do is when you lose weight, you enter our earn as you burn program. And by the end of the six weeks, however much weight you lose, or body fat percentage you lose, you get to stay at that rate. So if you lose 20, just give me an example. So if you lose 20 pounds, it's $20 off per month. So it's $108 a month. If you lose 4% body fat, then that would be the equivalent of losing 15 pounds, which means you get $15 off per month. Does that make sense? Awesome. Which one do you want to do? And then that's it. And just saying it with confidence. Because ultimately they really have no reason not to do it. If someone's financially strapped and they can drop 200 bucks or even $100 every other week, because it's an EFT at this point. And if they are not financially strapped, then some of them will just go for the credit only play. But most people will go for the EFT play depending on what the challenge is going for. If you offer it as cash back, people will take that more likely. If you offer it at a price point of 499 or 599, that will draw more people to the challenge. Once you go into 699, 799 price points, most people, and especially if you go credit only, more people will go towards the EFT. So it just depends on your needs. I would be set in your mind about which one you want to do, what your objective is, when you're using the green play. Because the only time people ever get confused is if you're confused. So if you don't know what you want them to do, they won't know what you want them to do. So I would just decide ahead of time, am I trying to get EFTs? Or do I want to get half EFTs and half cash? Or do I just want to catch? If you just want cash, then don't even mention the offer. If you want a transition play, then offer it at 599 with credit only. And the EFT is the other option, you'll get about 50-50. And then if you don't need that and just want to go all in on the green play, which is what I would recommend you do as soon as you're really possible, then offer it for 799 or 699 with credit only. And then the EFT as the other option, which most people will be attracted to. So that's more or less how this play works. Like, I can't tell you, it's so much about the assumption of the close. Like, it shouldn't feel like a sale anymore. It should feel like you're really giving them an awesome deal. Like, it should be a no-brainer. That's really the secret to this. And in the next video, I'll explain how some people are using little forms, which works well in large groups or even small groups, so that they can see it visually and then just circle which one they want. Okay? Awesome.
